Title: User Registration
Preconditions: User has internet access and a compatible device.
Test Data: Valid email address, strong password, user details (name, surname, date of birth).
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User selects the "Register" option.
3. User fills in the registration form with valid email, password, name, surname, and date of birth.
4. User confirms submission by clicking "Register."
Expected Result: User account is successfully created, and a confirmation message is displayed. User is redirected to the login screen.

Title: User Login
Preconditions: User has a registered account.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User selects the "Login" option.
3. User enters valid email and password.
4. User confirms submission by clicking "Login."
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in, and the application redirects to the home screen.

Title: Invalid Login Attempt
Preconditions: User has a registered account.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User selects the "Login" option.
3. User enters valid email and incorrect password.
4. User confirms submission by clicking "Login."
Expected Result: System displays an error message: "Invalid email or password. Please try again."

Title: Add New Food Item
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Name of food item, quantity, expiration date, storage location.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User navigates to the "Add Food" section.
3. User enters the name of the food item, quantity, expiration date, and storage location.
4. User confirms submission by clicking "Add."
Expected Result: Food item is successfully added to the user's inventory, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: View Food Inventory
Preconditions: User is logged in and has added at least one food item.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User navigates to the "Inventory" section.
Expected Result: All added food items are displayed with their details (name, quantity, expiration date, storage location).

Title: Update Food Item Details
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one food item in inventory.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User navigates to the "Inventory" section.
3. User selects a food item to update.
4. User modifies the quantity and expiration date.
5. User confirms submission by clicking "Update."
Expected Result: Food item details are successfully updated, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Delete Food Item
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one food item in inventory.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User navigates to the "Inventory" section.
3. User selects a food item to delete.
4. User confirms deletion by clicking "Delete."
Expected Result: Food item is successfully removed from the inventory, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Expiration Date Alert
Preconditions: User is logged in and has added at least one food item with an expiration date.
Test Data: Food item with expiration date set to tomorrow.
Test Steps:
1. User adds a food item with an expiration date set to tomorrow.
2. User logs out and waits until the next day.
3. User logs back in.
Expected Result: System displays a notification alerting the user about the expiring food item.

Title: Generate Food Waste Report
Preconditions: User is logged in and has added at least one food item that has expired.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User navigates to the "Reports" section.
3. User selects the option to generate a "Food Waste Report."
Expected Result: System generates and displays a report listing all expired food items, including their names, expiration dates, and quantities.

Title: Application Accessibility for Screen Readers
Preconditions: User has enabled screen reader on the device.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application with screen reader enabled.
2. User navigates through the main sections (Login, Register, Inventory).
Expected Result: Screen reader correctly reads out all interactive elements and navigation options. All text fields and buttons are properly labeled and accessible.

Title: Application Response Time
Preconditions: User has a stable internet connection.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the MyFood application.
2. User performs a common action (e.g., logging in, adding a food item).
Expected Result: The application responds within 2 seconds for all primary actions.

Title: Application Compatibility with Different Devices
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. User installs the MyFood application on an Android device.
2. User installs the MyFood application on an iOS device.
3. User logs in and performs basic actions (e.g., adding a food item, viewing inventory) on both devices.
Expected Result: The application functions correctly on both Android and iOS devices with no significant performance or display issues.

Title: Launch Screen Functionality
Preconditions: The app is installed on the device
Test Steps:
1. Open the app
2. Observe the splash screen with the app logo
3. Verify the login and registration buttons are visible
Expected Result: The splash screen displays the logo and provides options to login or register

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: User has a valid email and internet access
Test Data: Valid email, new password, personal details
Test Steps:
1. Click the registration button
2. Enter valid email, password, and personal details
3. Submit the form
Expected Result: User account is created and data is stored in the database

Title: Duplicate Email Registration Attempt

Test Data: Email already registered in the system
Test Steps:

3. Submit the form

Title: Registration While Already Authenticated

Expected Result: System redirects user to main page or login screen without showing registration

Title: Auto-Login via Token
Preconditions: User has previously logged in without logging out

2. Observe the login process
Expected Result: User is automatically logged in and directed to the main page

Title: Failed Auto-Login via Token
Preconditions: User has not logged in previously or session expired
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app
2. Observe the login process fails
Expected Result: System prompts user to login manually

Title: Successful Login via Email and Password
Preconditions: User has registered previously
Test Steps:
1. Click the login button
2. Enter valid email and password
3. Submit the form

Title: Invalid Credentials Login Attempt
Preconditions: User has registered previously

Title: Database Connection Error During Login

Title: Successful Login via Google Account

Title: Access Main Page After Login

2. Observe the main page

Title: Empty Frigorifero Warning

2. Click the search icon
3. Enter a search term to find a product
4. Select the product
5. Click the "vendi" (sell) button

Title: Modify Product in Frigorifero

Test Steps:

2. Select a product from the list.
3. Click the "modifica" (modify) button next to the product.

5. Confirm the changes.
Expected Result: The product details in the database are updated successfully.

Title: Sell Product from Frigorifero
Preconditions: At least one product must be present in the frigorifero.

Test Steps:
1. Open the frigorifero screen.
2. Select a product from the list.
3. Click the "vendi" (sell) button.
Expected Result: The product is added to the list of purchasable products.

Title: Add Product to Frigorifero
Preconditions: None (but requires a valid barcode and expiration date).
Test Data: A product with a valid barcode and expiration date.
Test Steps:
1. Click the "+" (add) button from the main screen or frigorifero screen.
2. Enter the product details (barcode, expiration date, and other required fields).
3. Confirm the submission.
Expected Result: The product is added to the frigorifero database.

Title: Remove Product from Frigorifero
Preconditions: At least one product must be present in the frigorifero.
Test Data: A product already in the frigorifero.
Test Steps:
1. Open the frigorifero screen.
2. Select a product from the list.
3. Click the "rimuovi" (remove) button.
4. Confirm the removal in the pop-up dialog.
Expected Result: The product is removed from the frigorifero database.

2. Type the nickname of another user in the search bar.

Title: Verify Firebase Database Security and Reliability
Preconditions: Firebase database is initialized and connected to the app
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to read, write, or delete data from the Firebase database without proper authentication.
2. Verify that unauthorized access attempts are blocked.
3. Simulate a network interruption during a database operation (e.g., adding a product).
4. Check if the app handles the interruption gracefully (e.g., retry mechanism, error message).
Expected Result:
1. All unauthorized access attempts are rejected.
2. No data is altered or exposed without proper authentication.
3. The app displays an appropriate error message and does not crash.
4. The operation resumes or completes successfully after reconnection.
--

Title: Verify Product Data Persistence in Firebase
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one product in the frigorifero
Test Data: A sample product (e.g., "Milk", expiry date: 2024-12-31, quantity: 2)
Test Steps:
1. Add a product to the frigorifero via the "Aggiungi prodotto" function.
2. Log out of the app and clear app data (or reinstall the app).
3. Log back in and navigate to the frigorifero section.
4. Verify the previously added product is still present.
Expected Result:
1. The product is successfully added to the frigorifero and the database.
2. The product data persists after logout and app data clearance.
3. The product appears in the frigorifero section upon relogin.
--

Title: Verify Token Credit Updates in Firebase
Preconditions: User has a balance of tokens (e.g., 100) in the app
Test Steps:
1. Purchase the product using tokens.
2. Verify the token balance updates in real-time in the app.
3. Check the Firebase database to confirm the token balance is deducted.
Expected Result:
1. The token balance decreases by 50 after purchase.
2. The updated balance is displayed correctly in the app.
3. The database reflects the new token balance.
--

Title: Verify App Functionality Without Internet Connection
Preconditions: App is installed and user is logged in
Test Steps:
1. Disable internet connection on the device.
2. Attempt to perform any app action requiring internet (e.g., add a product, view products, modify a product).
3. Check if the app displays an error message or disables the functionality.
Expected Result:
1. All actions requiring internet fail gracefully.
2. The app displays a clear error message (e.g., "No internet connection").
3. No crashes or unexpected behavior occur.
--

Title: Verify User Data Deletion (Right to Erasure)
Preconditions: User account exists with personal data, chat history, and at least one product listed for sale
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to account settings and select the "Delete Account" option.
2. Confirm the deletion request.
3. Verify the app prompts for final confirmation (e.g., "Are you sure you want to delete your account?").
4. After confirmation, check if all user data (profile, chat history, products) is removed from the app.
5. Log in to Firebase database and verify the user data is deleted.
Expected Result:
1. The app prompts for confirmation before deletion.
2. All user data, chat history, and products are removed from the app.
3. The Firebase database no longer contains the user’s data.
--

Title: Verify GDPR Compliance in Data Handling
Preconditions: User is logged in and has provided personal data (e.g., name, email)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the profile section and view personal data.
2. Attempt to export personal data (if applicable) or verify data is stored securely.
3. Check if the app provides options to correct or delete personal data.
4. Verify that data is encrypted during transmission and storage.
Expected Result:
1. Personal data is displayed securely and can be edited or deleted by the user.
2. The app does not expose sensitive data unnecessarily.
3. Data is encrypted in transit and at rest (if applicable).
--

Title: Verify App Response Time
Preconditions: Device has stable internet connection
Test Steps:
1. Measure the time taken for the app to load the homepage after login.
2. Measure the time taken to load a product detail page after selection.
3. Measure the time taken to add a product to the frigorifero.
Expected Result:
1. Homepage loads within 8 seconds.
2. Product detail page loads within 8 seconds.
3. Product addition completes within 8 seconds.
--

Title: Verify App Compatibility with Android 10+
Preconditions: Android emulator or device with Android 10+
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on an Android 10+ device/emulator.
2. Attempt to perform basic actions (e.g., login, navigate to frigorifero, add a product).
3. Verify the app functions without crashes or compatibility issues.
Expected Result:
1. The app installs successfully on Android 10+.
2. All basic functionalities work as expected.
3. No compatibility errors or crashes occur.
--

Title: Verify App Disinstallation Leaves No Residual Files
Preconditions: App is installed on the device
Test Steps:
1. Uninstall the app from the device.
2. Manually check the device storage for leftover files or folders related to the app.
3. Verify no residual data or cache is left behind.
Expected Result:
1. The app uninstalls completely without errors.
2. No leftover files or folders are found in the device storage.
--

Title: Verify Intuitiveness of Homepage Navigation
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the homepage
Test Steps:
1. Observe the homepage layout and available navigation options.
2. Attempt to access different sections (e.g., frigorifero, product listings) via buttons/icons.
3. Verify that the navigation is clear and requires minimal user effort.
Expected Result:
1. All navigation options are clearly labeled or icon-based.
2. The user can access all sections without confusion.
3. No unnecessary steps are required to navigate.
--

Title: Verify Frigorifero Functionality
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the homepage
Test Steps:
1. Click the frigorifero icon from the homepage.
2. Verify the frigorifero page loads with options to manage products.
3. Attempt to add a product (if no products exist) or verify existing products are displayed.
Expected Result:
1. The frigorifero page loads successfully.
2. Options to manage products (e.g., add, modify, delete) are visible.
3. Existing products (if any) are displayed correctly.
--

Title: Verify Product Detail Visualization
Preconditions: At least one product is listed in the frigorifero or search results
Test Steps:
1. Search for the product or navigate to it via the frigorifero.
2. Click on the product image to view details.
3. Verify all product information (name, expiry date, description, etc.) is displayed.
Expected Result:
1. The product detail page loads successfully.
2. All product information is displayed accurately.
3. No errors or missing data are present.
--

Title: Verify Product Selling Functionality
Preconditions: User has a product in the frigorifero
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the frigorifero and select the product to sell.
2. Click the "Metti in vendita" (Put up for sale) option.
3. Fill in the required details (e.g., price, description) and confirm.
4. Verify the product appears in the "In vendita" (For sale) section.
Expected Result:
1. The product is successfully put up for sale.
2. The product details page for sale includes all entered information.
3. The product is visible in the "In vendita" section.
--

Title: Verify Product Modification
Preconditions: User has a product in the frigorifero or for sale
Test Data: A sample product (e.g., "Cheese", current price: 30 tokens)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the product detail page.
2. Click the "Modifica" (Modify) button.
3. Change one or more details (e.g., price to 25 tokens, description).
4. Save the changes and verify the updates.
Expected Result:
1. The modification page loads with the current product details.
2. Changes are saved successfully.
3. The updated details are reflected in the product listing and database.
--

Title: Verify Product Addition to Frigorifero
Preconditions: User is on the homepage or frigorifero page
Test Steps:
1. Click the "+" button to add a product.
2. Fill in all required fields (name, expiry date, quantity).
3. Confirm the addition.
4. Verify the product appears in the frigorifero.
Expected Result:
1. The product addition form loads correctly.
2. The product is added successfully to the frigorifero.
3. A confirmation message or notification appears.
4. The product is visible in the frigorifero section.

Title: Remove Product from Fridge List
Preconditions: User is logged in and currently on the fridge page with at least one product listed.
Test Data: A product already added to the fridge list (e.g., "Milk").
Test Steps:
1. Locate the product "Milk" in the fridge list.
2. Click the "Rimuovi" (Remove) icon/button next to the product.
Expected Result: A visual notification confirming the removal of "Milk" appears, and the product is no longer visible in the fridge list.

Title: Verify Product Removal from Database
Preconditions: Product "Milk" has been removed from the fridge list (TC-FRIGORIFERO-001).
Test Steps: 1. Log in as another user (if multi-user testing is possible) or verify via backend/database check.
Expected Result: The product "Milk" is no longer accessible or visible to any user in the fridge list.

Title: Open Chat List from Main Page
Preconditions: User is on the main page and has at least one chat interaction.
Test Steps: 1. Click the "Messaggi" (Messages) icon on the main page.
Expected Result: The list of chats appears, showing "UserX" and other interactions.

Title: Open Selected Chat
Preconditions: User is on the chat list page with at least one chat (e.g., "UserX").
Test Steps: 1. Click on the chat with "UserX".
Expected Result: The chat page opens, displaying messages exchanged with "UserX".

Title: Navigate to Token Purchase Page
Preconditions: User is on the main page.
Test Steps: 1. Click the "Token" icon on the main page.
Expected Result: The token purchase page opens, showing available token options.

Title: Update User Balance After Token Purchase
Preconditions: User is on the token purchase page with a selected token amount (e.g., 10 tokens) and payment successful.
Test Data: Initial balance: 0 tokens; selected tokens: 10; payment method: valid.
Test Steps:
1. Select 10 tokens.
2. Complete the payment process.
Expected Result: The user’s balance updates to 10 tokens, and the new balance is displayed.

Title: Display Notification on Event
Preconditions: User has an active event triggering a notification (e.g., new message or product sale).
Test Steps: 1. Wait for or simulate an event (e.g., a new message arrives).
Expected Result: A notification appears on the screen.

Title: Open Specific Page from Notification
Preconditions: A notification is displayed (e.g., "New message from UserX").
Test Data: A notification with a clickable action.
Test Steps: 1. Click the notification.
Expected Result: The relevant page (e.g., chat with "UserX") opens.
